Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 84 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Building a Blog with Elixir and Phoenix 35 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 231 upvotes, 39 comments
  4. Personal Encyclopedias 720 upvotes, 146 comments
  5. Obsolete Sounds 182 upvotes, 35 comments
  6. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 29 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 236 upvotes, 72 comments
  8. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 806 upvotes, 281 comments
  9. Interoperability Can Save the Open Web (2023) 146 upvotes, 43 comments
  10. Doom entirely from DNS records 74 upvotes, 20 comments
  11. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 80 upvotes, 23 comments
  12. Stripe Projects: Provision and manage services from the CLI 50 upvotes, 13 comments
  13. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 427 upvotes, 206 comments
  14. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 405 upvotes, 200 comments
  15. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 176 upvotes, 82 comments
  16. End of "Chat Control": EU parliament stops mass surveillance 425 upvotes, 222 comments
  17. John Bradley, author of xv, has passed away 43 upvotes, 15 comments
  18. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 51 upvotes, 19 comments
  19. Niche Museums 96 upvotes, 42 comments
  20. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 56 upvotes, 22 comments
  21. Taming LLMs: Using Executable Oracles to Prevent Bad Code 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 115 upvotes, 55 comments
  23. ARC-AGI-3 481 upvotes, 316 comments
  24. Swift 6.3 281 upvotes, 181 comments
  25. Light on Glass: Why do you start making a game engine? 29 upvotes, 12 comments
  26. Fermented foods shaped human biology 20 upvotes, 10 comments
  27. Optimizing a lock-free ring buffer 73 upvotes, 65 comments
  28. Show HN: Orloj – agent infrastructure as code (YAML and GitOps) 8 upvotes, 4 comments
  29. Olympic Committee bars transgender athletes from women’s events 86 upvotes, 163 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
